title: CAA Verdict Contract
description: The pluggable interface of Continuous Architecture Assurance. Any reviewer (AI or otherwise) emits findings under this contract; free-form prose is not admissible. The contract is the product: reviewers, models, and the production substrate are substitutable implementations behind it.

reviewer.description: Stable identifier of the reviewer, e.g. 'failure-mode-analyst'.

reviewer_version.description: Semantic version of the reviewer prompt/skill. Used by Layer 4 to gate promotion: a new version ships only if it improves both recall and false-positive rate.

risk_class.description: Risk class of the change under review, set by the deterministic triage step (not by the reviewer).

verdict.description: Overall verdict. PASS requires an empty findings array.

findings.description: Zero or more findings. Each finding must be traceable to an origin (Gate G1).

category.description: Risk category. Recommended to use a fixed taxonomy (see experiments/) so Layer 4 recall can be matched by category rather than by vague mention.

evidence.description: Origin identifiers (EX-ID) or incident IDs that ground this finding. An empty list violates the 'no scenario without an origin' rule and is rejected as SCENARIO_WITHOUT_ORIGIN at Gate G1.

metric_that_proves_fix.description: The objective metric whose target value would demonstrate the fix works. A finding without this does not close (prevents decorative architecture).

reassess_when.description: Condition under which this decision must be revisited. Trips automatically from runtime telemetry (Layer 3 -> ADR feedback loop).
